pick.rationale: Virginia holds a marginal but consistent edge over Tennessee in every major efficiency metric — KenPom #13 (AdjEM +26.49) and Torvik #14 versus Tennessee's KenPom #15 (AdjEM +26.06) and Torvik #15 — with a superior offense ranked 28th nationally (AdjOE 122.2) compared to Tennessee's 35th, and a 29-4 record built as ACC Tournament runners-up under first-year coach Ryan Odom that reflects dramatically better late-season form than Tennessee's 22-11 mark with four losses in their final six regular-season games. The Cavaliers' dominant interior presence in Ugonna Onyenso — who set the ACC Tournament record with 21 blocks and was a force in wins over NC State and Miami en route to the final — creates a severe matchup problem for Tennessee's offense that the Volunteers have no credible answer for. Tennessee's key contributors Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie needed rest after the SEC Tournament exit, and while both are expected to play, the Vols' inconsistent late-season form and inferior résumé make Virginia the right side in what is otherwise a razor-thin matchup on raw efficiency numbers.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee vs. Virginia — Round of 32 Midwest Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia holds a narrow but consistent edge in efficiency ratings, a far superior record (29-4 vs. 22-11), dramatically better late-season form as ACC Tournament runners-up, and a dominant interior weapon in Ugonna Onyenso. Tennessee's inconsistent finish (4 losses in last 6 games) and inferior résumé tip this near-coin-flip to the Cavaliers.
evidence: KenPom: Virginia #13 (AdjEM +26.49) vs. Tennessee #15 (AdjEM +26.06); Torvik: Virginia #14 vs. Tennessee #15
Virginia AdjOE 122.2 (28th nationally), AdjDE 95.7 (14th); Tennessee AdjOE 121.1 (35th), AdjDE 95.0 (13th)
Virginia record 29-4, ACC Tournament runners-up (lost to #1 Duke 74-70 in final); Tennessee record 22-11, lost 4 of last 6 games, early SEC Tournament exit
Ugonna Onyenso set ACC Tournament record with 21 blocks including 9 in the championship game — elite interior presence with no comparable matchup answer from Tennessee
Tennessee's Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie needed rest after SEC Tournament; both expected to play but form and conditioning concerns remain
Virginia is a 3-seed vs. Tennessee's 6-seed, reflecting the résumé gap validated by the selection committee
